name: What local permitting differences matter between Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and Half Moon Bay?

mainEntity.name: How do permitting requirements differ across Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and Half Moon Bay, and what should I expect a general contractor to handle versus do myself?
mainEntity.text: How do permitting requirements differ across Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and Half Moon Bay, and what should I expect a general contractor to handle versus do myself?

acceptedAnswer.text: Even within San Mateo County and nearby cities, permitting rules and workflows differ enough that they can impact your schedule and costs. When you talk with a general contractor, ask how they typically handle permits in each city—Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and Half Moon Bay—and whether their base contract includes plan submittals and inspection coordination. You should ask which permits your project is likely to require: building, electrical, plumbing, mechanical, and others, plus whether zoning, coastal, or hazard‑zone reviews are triggered. Clarify how ADUs, second‑story additions, and major structural changes are treated across these jurisdictions and whether separate planning approvals precede building permits. Discuss who prepares the permit drawings and supporting documents. In many cases, an architect or engineer will lead design while the contractor manages submittals and inspection scheduling, but responsibilities can vary. Finally, ask about typical review times in each city, common reasons plans get returned for corrections, and how the contractor communicates permit status to you. Insist that permit handling and related fees be spelled out in the contract so you know what is included, what is billed as extra, and where you may need to step in, such as signing forms or attending hearings.
acceptedAnswer.description: Permitting rules vary by city. Expect your general contractor to identify required permits, coordinate with designers, submit plans, and schedule inspections, but confirm this in writing. Ask specifically how Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and Half Moon Bay differ on structural work, additions, ADUs, and coastal or hazard‑zone reviews so you understand timelines and responsibilities.
